user_query:
Read input/queries.json (and input/instructions.md if present). For each query:
- Use web search to find fresh, credible information.
- Save raw results to output/raw_results.jsonl as one JSON object per query with this exact schema: {"query": string, "fetched_at": ISO8601 timestamp (UTC, e.g., 2026-04-19T12:34:56Z), "results": [up to 5 unique items, each {"title": string, "url": string (http/https), "content": string snippet}]}. Deduplicate by URL and include only publicly accessible sources.
- Then write a concise analyst-style summary to output/report.md with:
  1) A top-level section "## Methodology" describing how you searched and vetted sources.
  2) One section per query titled exactly "## {query}". Under each, include 3–5 bullet points summarizing the main findings ("- " bullets only), then a sub-section titled "### Sources" listing at least 2 distinct source URLs that also appear in output/raw_results.jsonl for that query.
Do not use paywalled sources. Keep the report focused and cite URLs directly under each query’s Sources section.
